name: Gwangjang Market Bindaetteok
description: Famous traditional Korean mung bean pancakes at Gwangjang Market. A must-visit for authentic street food experience.

pros_cons.pros: Authentic street food atmosphere, incredibly affordable, English menu available
pros_cons.cons: Can be crowded on weekends, mostly cash only, limited seating

name: Tosokchon Samgyetang
description: Legendary ginseng chicken soup restaurant near Gyeongbokgung Palace. Queue is long but absolutely worth it.

pros_cons.pros: Legendary flavor, near Gyeongbokgung, English menu, great for winter
pros_cons.cons: Long queues, slightly pricey for soup, closes early

name: Jungsik
description: Michelin-starred modern Korean cuisine. Innovative dishes that reinterpret traditional Korean flavors.

pros_cons.pros: Michelin-starred, innovative menu, English-speaking staff, stunning plating
pros_cons.cons: Very expensive, reservation required weeks in advance, dress code

name: Myeongdong Kyoja
description: Iconic noodle and dumpling restaurant in Myeongdong. Famous for kalguksu (knife-cut noodles) and mandu.

pros_cons.pros: Very affordable, English menu, iconic local experience, central location
pros_cons.cons: Always crowded, no reservations, plain interior

name: Maple Tree House
description: Premium Korean BBQ with top-quality beef galbi and pork belly. Great for groups. English-speaking staff.

pros_cons.pros: Premium quality meat, English-speaking staff, great atmosphere for groups
pros_cons.cons: Expensive, requires ventilation (clothes smell after), reservation recommended

name: Sura Korean Royal Cuisine
description: Experience authentic Korean royal court cuisine in a beautiful hanok setting near Insadong.

pros_cons.pros: Authentic royal cuisine experience, beautiful hanok setting, English menu
pros_cons.cons: Pricey, slow service, must reserve in advance

name: Okonomi Itaewon
description: Authentic Japanese savory pancakes made right in front of you. Cozy atmosphere, friendly staff.

pros_cons.pros: Authentic okonomiyaki, fun table-side cooking, English menu
pros_cons.cons: Small space, can feel smoky, limited seating

name: Ramen Nagi Seoul
description: Popular Japanese ramen chain. Rich tonkotsu broth, customizable spice level. Always a queue but moves fast.

pros_cons.pros: Rich broth, customizable, fast queue, English menu
pros_cons.cons: Queue can be long on weekends, small space, no reservations

name: Sushi Cho
description: Intimate omakase sushi experience. Chef sources fresh fish daily. Reservations essential.

pros_cons.pros: Exceptional freshness, intimate experience, world-class chef
pros_cons.cons: No English menu, very expensive, reservations required months ahead

name: Tartine Bakery Seoul
description: SF's famous Tartine Bakery in Seoul. Excellent sourdough, pastries, and brunch items. Great for morning visits.

pros_cons.pros: World-famous bread, great coffee, relaxed atmosphere, English menu
pros_cons.cons: Pricey for a bakery, sells out quickly, can be crowded on weekends

name: Vatos Urban Tacos
description: Korean-Mexican fusion tacos. Try the kimchi carnitas burrito. Lively atmosphere, great cocktails.

pros_cons.pros: Unique Korean-Mexican fusion, great cocktails, English menu, lively vibe
pros_cons.cons: Can be noisy, waits on weekends, not the most authentic Mexican

name: The Burger Club
description: Gourmet burgers made with premium Korean beef. Thick patties, fresh ingredients, great fries.

pros_cons.pros: Quality Korean beef, great fries, English menu, easy to find
pros_cons.cons: Average price for a burger, nothing particularly unique

name: Gorilla in the Kitchen
description: Seoul's pioneer healthy restaurant. Organic ingredients, vegan-friendly, beautiful plating. Great for health-conscious travelers.

pros_cons.pros: Organic ingredients, vegan-friendly, beautiful presentation, English menu
pros_cons.cons: Expensive, small portions, limited seating

name: Plant Cafe & Kitchen
description: 100% plant-based menu. Surprisingly satisfying vegan versions of classic dishes. Warm, welcoming atmosphere.

pros_cons.pros: 100% vegan, great value, English menu, warm atmosphere
pros_cons.cons: Itaewon can be hard to find, inconsistent quality at times

name: Dadam Korean Temple Food
description: Authentic Korean Buddhist temple cuisine. No meat, no garlic, no onion. Pure flavors, peaceful ambiance.

pros_cons.pros: Truly unique experience, healthy and pure ingredients, near Insadong sights
pros_cons.cons: No garlic or onion (unusual taste), closes early, limited menu

name: Din Tai Fung Seoul
description: World-famous Taiwanese dumpling chain. Perfectly crafted xiaolongbao (soup dumplings). Consistent quality.

pros_cons.pros: World-class dumplings, consistent quality, English menu, great for groups
pros_cons.cons: Long waits, pricey for Chinese food, not uniquely Korean

name: Hongkong Banjum
description: Classic Korean-Chinese cuisine. Must-try jajangmyeon (black bean noodles) and jjamppong (spicy seafood noodles).

pros_cons.pros: Very affordable, authentic Korean-Chinese taste, quick service
pros_cons.cons: No English menu, plain atmosphere, nothing fancy

name: Noona's Ice Cream
description: Creative Asian-inspired ice cream flavors like black sesame, taro, and matcha. Perfect for hot days.

pros_cons.pros: Unique Asian flavors, affordable, Instagram-friendly, English menu
pros_cons.cons: Small space, can sell out of popular flavors, limited seating

name: Seoulero Bingsu Cafe
description: Traditional Korean shaved ice dessert with various toppings. Injeolmi (rice cake) bingsu is the crowd favorite.

pros_cons.pros: Authentic Korean dessert, very affordable, English menu, near Insadong sights
pros_cons.cons: Only seasonal (best in summer), can be too sweet, basic interior

name: Cafe Bora
description: Famous for its purple soft serve ice cream and matcha drinks. Extremely photogenic — great for Instagram.

pros_cons.pros: Iconic Instagram spot, unique purple color, affordable, English menu
pros_cons.cons: Always crowded, queue can be 30+ minutes, trendy over substance
